name: BlackVM
author: blackredit@github.com
description:
BlackVM – Run full KVM/QEMU virtual machines on Pterodactyl.

The Docker image (ghcr.io/blackredit/blackvm) already contains:
• QEMU system x86_64
• OVMF (UEFI firmware)
• Legacy BIOS
• netboot.xyz ISO (network installer)
• VirtIO-Win ISO (Windows drivers)
• noVNC browser remote desktop
• All helper scripts (bvm-snapshot, bvm-resize, bvm-monitor, bvm-status)

The installation script ONLY creates the disk image and VNC password file.

Features:
• KVM hardware acceleration (Intel VT-x / AMD-V)
• UEFI (OVMF) and Legacy BIOS
• Primary + optional secondary QCOW2 disk
• Secondary ISO slot (VirtIO drivers, rescue ISO, etc.)
• VirtIO disk, VirtIO network, sound, USB 3.0
• Memory ballooning
• CPU topology (sockets / cores / threads)
• CPU limit via cpulimit
• QEMU Guest Agent
• noVNC browser-based remote desktop
• Offline snapshots (bvm-snapshot)
• Disk resize (bvm-resize)
• QEMU monitor helper (bvm-monitor)

© 2026 blackredit – github.com/blackredit/blackvm

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================
#  BlackVM – Installation Script
#  The Docker image already contains EVERYTHING.
#  This script only creates the disk image + VNC password.
# ============================================================
set -e

apt-get update -y -qq
apt-get install -y -qq qemu-utils

cd /mnt/server

DISK_FILE="${DISK_FILE:-disk.qcow2}"
DISK_SIZE="${DISK_SIZE:-100G}"
VNC_PASSWORD="${VNC_PASSWORD:-black1234}"

echo "[BlackVM] Setting up server..."
echo ""

# Primary disk
if [ ! -f "${DISK_FILE}" ]; then
    echo "[+] Creating primary disk: ${DISK_FILE} (${DISK_SIZE})"
    qemu-img create -f qcow2 "${DISK_FILE}" "${DISK_SIZE}"
else
    echo "[i] Disk '${DISK_FILE}' already exists – skipping"
fi

# Optional secondary disk
if [ -n "${SECONDARY_DISK}" ] && [ ! -f "${SECONDARY_DISK}" ]; then
    SECONDARY_DISK_SIZE="${SECONDARY_DISK_SIZE:-50G}"
    echo "[+] Creating secondary disk: ${SECONDARY_DISK} (${SECONDARY_DISK_SIZE})"
    qemu-img create -f qcow2 "${SECONDARY_DISK}" "${SECONDARY_DISK_SIZE}"
fi

# VNC password file
echo "[+] Writing VNC password..."
echo 'change vnc password' > qemu_cmd.txt
echo "${VNC_PASSWORD}" >> qemu_cmd.txt
chmod 600 qemu_cmd.txt

# Shared folder
mkdir -p shared
chmod 777 shared

echo ""
echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║   BlackVM installation complete!           ║"
echo "╠════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
echo "║  Disk      : ${DISK_FILE} (${DISK_SIZE})"
echo "║  VNC pass  : ${VNC_PASSWORD}"
echo "║  noVNC     : http://NODE-IP:SERVER_PORT"
echo "║"
echo "║  ⚠ Ensure /dev/kvm is mounted on this node"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

variables.name: noVNC Web Port
variables.description: Port for the noVNC browser interface. Must match an allocated port in Pterodactyl.

variables.name: VNC Password
variables.description: Password for VNC / noVNC access. Change before first use!

variables.name: Guest RAM (MB)
variables.description: Memory allocated to the VM in megabytes. Linux: 1024+ recommended. Windows: 4096+ required.

variables.name: Enable KVM
variables.description: 1 = hardware KVM acceleration (requires /dev/kvm mount). 0 = TCG software emulation (slow, no mount needed).

variables.name: Machine Type
variables.description: QEMU chipset: 'q35' (modern, PCIe, recommended) or 'pc' (legacy i440fx).

variables.name: Enable UEFI (OVMF)
variables.description: 1 = UEFI boot (recommended for Windows 11 and modern Linux). 0 = Legacy BIOS.

variables.name: Primary Disk File
variables.description: Filename of the main QCOW2 disk image in /home/container.

variables.name: Primary Disk Size (install only)
variables.description: Size of the primary disk created during installation. Has no effect on existing disks. Examples: 80G, 200G, 500G.

variables.name: Disk Cache Mode
variables.description: Disk I/O cache mode (applies when VirtIO is off). writeback=fast, writethrough=safe, none=best with USE_VIRTIO=1.

variables.name: Secondary Disk File
variables.description: Optional second QCOW2 disk (attached as VirtIO). Leave blank to disable. Created automatically on install if specified.

variables.name: Secondary Disk Size (install only)
variables.description: Size of the secondary disk created during installation if SECONDARY_DISK is set.

variables.name: Primary ISO / CD-ROM
variables.description: Boot ISO filename. Default: netboot.xyz.iso (pre-installed in image). Leave blank for disk-only boot.

variables.name: Secondary ISO (Drivers)
variables.description: Second CD-ROM slot. Set to 'virtio-win.iso' for Windows VirtIO drivers (already in image). Leave blank to disable.

variables.name: Enable VirtIO Disk
variables.description: 1 = high-performance VirtIO disk (use on Linux or after VirtIO driver install on Windows). 0 = safe IDE emulation (always works – use for Windows install).

variables.name: Enable VirtIO Network
variables.description: 1 = fast VirtIO NIC. 0 = legacy Realtek RTL8139 (use for Windows install, enable after VirtIO driver install).

variables.name: Port Forwarding Rules
variables.description: QEMU user-mode network port forwards. Format: hostfwd=tcp::HOST-:GUEST (comma-separated).

variables.name: CPU Sockets
variables.description: Virtual CPU socket count (usually 1).

variables.name: CPU Cores per Socket
variables.description: Number of virtual CPU cores per socket.

variables.name: CPU Threads per Core
variables.description: CPU threads per core. Set to 2 to simulate Hyper-Threading / SMT.

variables.name: Enable Memory Balloon
variables.description: 1 = VirtIO balloon driver allows host to reclaim unused guest RAM dynamically. Requires balloon driver inside the VM.

variables.name: Enable VirtIO GPU
variables.description: 1 = VirtIO GPU with EGL headless rendering (experimental, requires host GPU passthrough). 0 = standard emulated VGA.

variables.name: Enable Sound Card
variables.description: 1 = emulated Intel HDA sound card. 0 = no sound (less overhead for headless servers).

variables.name: Enable USB 3.0 Controller
variables.description: 1 = xHCI USB 3.0 controller + USB tablet (precise mouse). 0 = legacy USB tablet only.

variables.name: Enable QEMU Guest Agent
variables.description: 1 = adds virtio-serial for QEMU Guest Agent (graceful shutdown, guest info). Requires qemu-guest-agent inside the VM.

variables.name: Shared Directory
variables.description: Path to expose as a FAT32 drive inside the VM. Example: /home/container/shared. Leave blank to disable.

variables.name: Extra QEMU Arguments
variables.description: Raw QEMU arguments appended to the command. Advanced use only. Example: -monitor unix:/tmp/bvm.sock,server,nowait
